summary: Joel shares links related to the arsDigita collapse, noting that while incompetent VCs are blamed for destroying the company, the decline in demand for internet consulting was equally important. He also links to Aaron Swartz's parody of Edward Tufte's anti-PowerPoint book.
key_points: The arsDigita collapse was not solely due to VC mismanagement - market demand for internet consulting had also plummeted
Links to an insider account of the arsDigita saga for those following the story
References Aaron Swartz's parody of Tufte's anti-PowerPoint arguments
money_quotes: Much overlooked in the fact that incompetent VCs destroyed the company was the fact that demand for the company's product (Internet consulting) had plummeted.
key_insight: Company failures attributed to bad management often have a simpler underlying cause: the market for their product disappeared.
